briefdescription.para: SimulationManager clock, updated once per slice.
current update slice
number of pending steps
number of requested steps in current simulation
update clock_+from_step<=T<clock_+to_step_
update clock_+from_step<=T<clock_+to_step_
Wall-clock time at the begin of a time slice.
Wall-clock time at the end of time slice.
Accumulated wall-clock time spent simulating (in us)
Indicates whether the SimulationManager is in a prepared state.
true if simulation in progress
indicates whether the SimulationManager has already been simulated for sometime
true after exception during update_ simulation must not be resumed
Indicates whether time should be printed during simulations (or not)
Indicates wheter waveform relaxation is used.
Desired waveform relaxation communication interval (in ms)
Convergence tolerance of waveform relaxation method.
maximal number of iterations used for waveform relaxation
interpolation order for waveform relaxation method
throw exception if single update cycle takes longer than update_time_limit_ (seconds, default inf)
shortest update time seen so far (seconds)
longest update time seen so far (seconds)
Prepare manager for operation.

detaileddescription.para: After this method has completed, the manager should be completely initialized and "ready for action".

simplesect.para: Initialization of any given manager may depend on other managers having been initialized before. KernelManager::initialize() is responsible for calling the initialization routines on the specific managers in correct order.

briefdescription.para: Take down manager after operation.

detaileddescription.para: After this method has completed, all dynamic data structures created by the manager shall be deallocated and containers emptied. Plain variables need not be reset.

simplesect.para: Finalization of any given manager may depend on other managers not having been finalized yet. KernelManager::finalize() is responsible for calling the initialization routines on the specific managers in correct order, i.e., the opposite order of initialize() calls.

briefdescription.para: Check for errors in time before run.
Initialize simulation for a set of run calls.

detaileddescription.para: Must be called before a sequence of runs, and again after cleanup.

briefdescription.para: Run a simulation for another Time.

detaileddescription.para: Can be repeated ad infinitum with calls to get_status(), but any changes to the network are undefined, leading serious risk of incorrect results.

briefdescription.para: Closes a set of runs, doing finalizations such as file closures.

detaileddescription.para: After cleanup() is called, no more run()s can be called before another prepare() call.

briefdescription.para: Returns true if waveform relaxation is used.
Get the desired communication interval for the waveform relaxation.
Get the convergence tolerance of the waveform relaxation method.
Get the interpolation order of the waveform relaxation method.
Get the time at the beginning of the current time slice.
Get the time at the beginning of the previous time slice.
Precise time of simulation.

simplesect.para: The precise time of the simulation is defined only while the simulation is not in progress.

briefdescription.para: Return true, if the SimulationManager has already been simulated for some time.

detaileddescription.para: This does NOT indicate that simulate has been called (i.e. if Simulate is called with 0 as argument, the flag is still set to false.)

briefdescription.para: Return true, if the SimulationManager has been prepared for simulation.

detaileddescription.para: This is the case from the time when the Prepare function is called until the simulation context is left by a call to Cleanup.

briefdescription.para: Get slice number.

detaileddescription.para: Increased by one for each slice. Can be used to choose alternating buffers.

briefdescription.para: Return current simulation time.
Get the simulation duration in the current call to run().
Get the start time of the current call to run().
Get the simulation's time at the end of the current call to run().
Return start of current time slice, in steps.
Return end of current time slice, in steps.
Sorts source table and connections and create new target table.
Set time measurements for internal profiling to zero (reg.
Set time measurements for internal profiling to zero (reg.
actually run simulation, aka wrap update_
actually perform simulation
Update time to next time step.
TODO: Remove, replace by logging!
